assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 14: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Modified test file has both additive signals (new dedup test, stronger assertions) and reductive signals (removed qualifier test); new test file is purely additive |'. The MIXED verdict is present with explicit mention of both additive and reductive signals.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: Report Test Change Classification Details, structural summary: '+1 test function (`test_recommend_purls_dedup`), -1 test function (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`)'. The semantic assessment further states: 'The removed test (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`) verified qualifier-specific behavior that no longer exists after this feature change.' The removal is explicitly identified as a reductive structural signal contributing to the MIXED classification.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers
assertion_results.evidence: The structural summary lists '+2 assertions in `test_recommend_purls_basic` (two `!contains("?")` checks)' — treating the changes as additive, not reductive. The Test Requirements Details row 1 states 'Assertions changed from fully-qualified PURL to `pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12` plus `!contains("?")` checks' — this describes the change but does not identify it as a relaxation or reductive signal. The relaxation of the assertion (from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to a versioned PURL without qualifiers) is never flagged as a reductive finding in the structural summary or elsewhere in the test change classification.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: Report Test Change Classification Details: '**New file: `tests/api/purl_simplify.rs`** - 3 new test functions, ~9 new assertions - Purely additive; covers edge cases for the simplified format'. The file is explicitly recognized as additive with 3 new test functions.

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: Report Test Change Classification Details structural summary: '+1 test function (`test_recommend_purls_dedup`)'. Also report line 14 in the summary table: 'additive signals (new dedup test, stronger assertions)'. The function is explicitly recognized as additive.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). All output content is analysis and reporting. No evidence of any file modification, write operations, or code changes in any of the output files. The report states findings and presents them for review without modifying source or test files.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and presents detailed tables of findings including Acceptance Criteria Details, Test Requirements Details, Test Change Classification Details, Scope Analysis, and Security Scan. There is no merge action, no merge command, and no indication of automated merging anywhere in the report. The report ends with '*This report was AI-generated by [sdlc-workflow/verify-pr]...*' — presenting findings for human review.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification is produced by the Style/Conventions sub-agent (which spawns a test classification sub-agent for modified files) — the classification verdict is attributed to the Style/Conventions domain in the report
assertion_results.evidence: The Test Change Classification row appears in the main verification report table (report.md line 14) alongside other checks (Scope Containment, Diff Size, CI Status, etc.) without any attribution to a 'Style/Conventions' domain or sub-agent. The detailed Test Change Classification section (lines 44-59) also contains no reference to Style/Conventions or any sub-agent attribution. The classification is presented as a standalone check with no domain attribution.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: The structural summary is file-content-based: '+1 test function, -1 test function, +2 assertions, -4 assertions from removed test, +3 assertions from added test, Net assertion change: +1'. However, the semantic assessment violates constraint 1.18 by referencing task requirements: 'Its removal is intentional and aligned with the task description' and 'Coverage intent has shifted to match the new feature behavior rather than being lost.' These phrases reference the task description and feature behavior rather than being strictly based on test file content comparison.
